title: CAHO appoints Dr Ilankumaran Kaliamoorthy as Chairperson of Healthcare Institutions Division
summary: Apollo Hospitals executive to lead quality and accreditation initiatives for 2026–2028 term
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Apollo Hospitals executive to lead quality and accreditation initiatives for 2026–2028 term

Consortium of Accredited Healthcare Organizations announced the appointment of Dr Ilankumaran Kaliamoorthy, Chief Executive Officer, Apollo Hospitals Chennai Region, as Chairperson of its Healthcare Institutions Division for the 2026–2028 term.

CAHO is a body focused on patient safety and quality standards across healthcare institutions in India and brings together accredited hospitals and healthcare providers to support practices, clinical governance, and system-level processes.

In his role, Dr Kaliamoorthy will work with member institutions to strengthen quality frameworks, promote standardisation of care, and enable knowledge-sharing across the healthcare ecosystem. The role will focus on approaches to accreditation, patient safety, and clinical care across care settings.

Dr Kaliamoorthy has experience in hospital administration and clinical operations. At Apollo Hospitals, Chennai Region, he has led initiatives related to clinical outcomes, operational processes, and patient care models.

Dr Ilankumaran Kaliamoorthy said, “This is an important moment for healthcare in India, where the focus is increasingly shifting towards measurable quality, patient safety, and accountability across institutions. CAHO has played a critical role in shaping this agenda, and I look forward to working with member organisations to strengthen systems that deliver consistent, high-quality care. The priority will be to translate standards into everyday practice and support institutions in building capabilities that are both scalable and sustainable.”

The appointment comes at a time when healthcare systems in India are placing focus on accreditation, transparency, and quality improvement, with organisations such as CAHO supporting alignment across institutions.
